steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes what the tracking method does to sidewalks that few pedestrians use. The result of this action is incomplete maps, so the blank must mean the method fails to include or account for those sidewalks.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'neglects': means fails to account for or overlooks — the method overlooks low-traffic sidewalks, leaving them off the map. ✓
  • Choice B — 'oversees': means supervises or manages — makes no sense in context. ✗
  • Choice C — 'approves': means endorses — unrelated to mapping or omission. ✗
  • Choice D — 'detects': means finds or identifies — the opposite of the problem; if the method detected those sidewalks, the maps would be complete. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
